content:
I have read all 10 files in their entirety. Here is a comprehensive summary organized by the categories you requested:

---

## 📁 FILE-BY-FILE SUMMARIES

### 1. PORTFOLIO_HANDOFF.md (187 lines, 6.8 KB)
Top-level handoff document for Joel Alfred Israel's ThinkPad Terminal Portfolio. Contains:
- **Quick start**: `npm run dev` → localhost:4321, `npx astro build` for production
- **Architecture flow**: User opens page → LaptopShell.astro (3D CSS) → lid open animation → BootOverlay.tsx (POST boot) → Terminal.tsx (xterm.js)
- **Complete file map**: 24 source files (~131 KB), 26 doc files (~335 KB)
- **Known issues table**: 6 items (unused `_owner` variable, no `.gitignore`, no deployment config, no favicon/OG, portfolio.json needs AgentOS project, legacy git files)
- **Priority task list**: P1=Deploy (Vercel), P2=Content updates, P3=Polish (mobile, a11y, Lighthouse)
- **Dependencies**: Astro 5.7, React 19, xterm 5.5, TypeScript 5.7
- **User profile**: Joel Alfred Israel, 2nd-year CS @ VIT Chennai, CGPA 8.09/10, published researcher, 8+ languages
- **Related project**: AgentOS at `d:\agentos`

### 2. ARCHITECTURE.md (413 lines, 13 KB)
Detailed system architecture with Mermaid diagrams:
- **Three layers**: Static shell (zero JS), React islands (client-side), Terminal engine (pure TS)
- **Key decision**: Separation of presentation (React) from logic (TypeScript modules) — terminal engine is framework-agnostic
- **Rendering pipeline**: Strict boot sequence with ~5.5s total boot time (pre-boot 400ms → boot lines ~2.5s → post-boot 600ms → fade-out 550ms → lid open 1.2s → terminal fade-in 300ms)
- **Island hydration**: BootOverlay + Terminal = `client:load`; AudioBar = `client:idle`; LaptopShell/CRT/Layout = static (zero JS)
- **Data flow**: portfolio.json → data.ts (cached loader) → commands.ts/filesystem.ts/chatbot.ts. AI chatbot uses Xiaomi MiMo API (OpenAI-compatible format)
- **Terminal engine layers**: Terminal.tsx → commands.ts (interpreter) → feature modules (filesystem, chatbot, neofetch, snake, themes) → shared infra (config, types, data, easter)
- **TerminalWriter abstraction**: Commands never touch xterm.js directly — testable without a browser
- **CSS architecture**: 5 layered CSS files with strict import order (global → laptop → crt → terminal → boot). CSS variables for design tokens
- **Build**: Static output, Vite-optimized xterm deps, TS path aliases (@components, @terminal, @styles, @data), hashed bundles in dist/_astro/

### 3. components.md (361 lines, 11.5 KB)
Component-level reference documentation:
- **Layout.astro**: Base HTML wrapper with SEO, Google Fonts (Share Tech Mono, JetBrains Mono), CSS imports. Props: `title`, `description`
- **LaptopShell.astro**: 3D laptop frame, pure HTML/CSS, zero JS. `#lid` gets `.open` class via JS. Keyboard/trackpad are decorative (`aria-hidden`)
- **CRTScreen.astro**: CRT effects — barrel distortion (SVG `feDisplacementMap`), scanlines (repeating gradient + mix-blend-mode), vignette (radial gradient), pulsing amber LED. Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`
- **BootOverlay.tsx**: BIOS-style boot with 15 typewriter-animated lines. Uses `requestAnimationFrame`. Calls `onComplete()` after fade-out. CSS classes: `ok` (green), `dim` (gray), `info` (amber)
- **Terminal.tsx**: Main xterm.js wrapper with FitAddon, WebLinksAddon, input buffering, command history (80 entries, deduplicates), tab completion, FIGlet banner, seasonal greeting, async command execution. Prompt: `joel01010@portfolio:~$`
- **AudioBar.tsx**: Fixed bottom-right audio toggle (🔊/🔇). Lazy init on first interaction, 0.18 volume, looping `/sounds/ambient.mp3`
- **Terminal engine modules**: config.ts (constants), types.ts (interfaces), themes.ts (amber/green themes), commands.ts (dispatcher), filesystem.ts (virtual ls/cd/cat), neofetch.ts, chatbot.ts (MiMo API), easter.ts (Konami code + seasonal), snake.ts (canvas-based)

### 4. CONTRIBUTING.md (339 lines, 9.9 KB)
Full contribution guidelines:
- **Prerequisites**: Node.js ≥18, npm ≥9
- **Branch strategy**: main (prod) ← feat/*, fix/*, docs/*, refactor/*
- **Commit convention**: Conventional Commits (feat/fix/docs/style/refactor)
- **No automated tests**: Manual testing checklist provided (10 steps including boot sequence, all commands, tab completion, history, Ctrl+C, Konami code, snake game, responsive resize, build)
- **Coding standards**: Strict TS (no `any`, use `unknown`), path aliases required, function components only, JSDoc on all exports, vanilla CSS only (no Tailwind/CSS-in-JS/SASS), `rem` for font sizes, `px` for borders
- **ANSI color codes**: Full reference for amber, gold, light amber, dim gray, red, green, cyan, bold, reset
- **File naming**: kebab-case for .ts/.css/.json, PascalCase for .astro/.tsx components
- **Architecture rules**: (1) Terminal engine must never import React/DOM, (2) Commands use TerminalWriter abstraction, (3) Data goes through data.ts, (4) Config lives in config.ts, (5) Styles stay in their CSS layer
- **Adding features checklists**: New command, new theme, new Astro component, new easter egg — each with step-by-step
- **PR template**: What/Why/How/Testing/Screenshots sections
- **Documentation requirements**: JSDoc for all exports, `@fileoverview` module headers, update docs when behavior changes

### 5. MAINTENANCE.md (497 lines, 12.4 KB)
Step-by-step maintenance playbook with 12 recipes:
- **Update portfolio data**: Edit portfolio.json, verify commands + filesystem + neofetch, sync config.ts if owner changes
- **Add a new project**: Just add to portfolio.json projects array — filesystem, commands, chatbot, neofetch auto-update
- **Add a new command**: 4-step process (handler → COMMANDS map → CONFIG.commands → help text) with verification checklist
- **Add a new theme**: Define ITheme with all 16 ANSI colors → register in themes record
- **Update Ezra's AI model**: Change config.ts endpoint/model/key — any OpenAI-compatible API works (OpenAI, Groq, Together AI, local Ollama). ⚠️ API key currently client-side
- **Update Ezra's personality**: Edit ezra_system.md (plain text system prompt, portfolio data auto-injected)
- **Change boot sequence**: Edit BOOT_LINES array in BootOverlay.tsx, adjust timing in config.ts
- **Update neofetch**: Edit ASCII_ART (28 chars wide, 14 lines) and infoLines array
- **Add easter egg**: Seasonal greetings or new key sequences with DOM listeners
- **Change fonts**: Update Google Fonts link, global.css variable, Terminal.tsx xterm config
- **Update dependencies**: npm outdated/update, watch for xterm/Astro/React major version breaks
- **Deploy**: `npm run build` → Vercel (recommended), Netlify, or manual static upload. Post-deploy checklist (8 items)
- **Troubleshooting**: 7 common issues with fixes (boot not starting, terminal not appearing, data not loaded, Ezra errors, xterm not fitting, CRT effects missing, build TS errors)

### 6. TECH_DEBT.md (339 lines, 10.6 KB)
Comprehensive tech debt tracker with priority levels:

**P0 — Critical (2 items):**
1. **API Key exposed client-side**: `mimoApiKey` hardcoded in config.ts, extractable from JS bundle. Fix: serverless proxy (Vercel/Netlify Edge Function), requires switching to `output: 'hybrid'`
2. **No input sanitization for chatbot**: User input passed directly to AI API. Fix: input length limits (500 chars), content filtering, rate limiting (2s interval)

**P1 — Important (5 items):**
1. **No automated tests**: Zero test coverage despite highly testable pure TS engine. Fix: Vitest for filesystem, commands, data, themes
2. **Duplicate TerminalTheme interface**: Defined in both types.ts and themes.ts with different fields. Fix: consolidate to types.ts
3. **No streaming for AI responses**: Full completion wait with "thinking..." delay. Fix: SSE streaming via ReadableStream
4. **Left/right arrow keys not supported**: No cursor movement within input line. Medium complexity fix
5. **No mobile support**: No touch input, 3D laptop doesn't scale. Options: responsive CSS, "best on desktop" message, or on-screen keyboard

**P2 — Nice-to-have (6 items):**
1. No command history persistence (fix: localStorage)
2. No loading state for portfolio data
3. Boot sequence can't be skipped (fix: click/key to skip, or localStorage for repeat visitors)
4. No dark mode favicon
5. Duplicate CONFIG export (both `CONFIG` and `config`)
6. No Error Boundaries for React islands
7. CRT filter performance on low-end devices
8. No robots.txt or sitemap.xml

**Improvement roadmap**: 4 phases — Security (P0) → Testing (P1) → UX Polish (P1-P2) → Performance & SEO (P2)

**Performance stats**: First Paint ~200ms (optimal), TTI ~5.5s (boot animation), JS bundle ~200KB gzip (xterm dominant), Google Fonts blocks render

### 7. AGENTS.md (361 lines, 12.5 KB)
Guide specifically for AI coding agents:
- **Project quick reference table**: Astro 5 + React 19, TS 5.7 strict, xterm 5.5, static output, npm, Node ≥18
- **8 critical rules**: (1) Never modify legacy/, (2) Never import React in src/terminal/, (3) Never use `any`, (4) Always update CONFIG.commands, (5) Preserve `as const`, (6) Never hardcode values, (7) Never change CSS import order, (8) Never use document/window at module top level
- **File map**: Most-edited files vs rarely-touched files
- **Common tasks with code examples**: Add command, add project, change colors, change ASCII banner, add filesystem directory
- **TypeScript conventions**: `import type` for types, CommandHandler signature, null-check data access pattern
- **ANSI color reference table**: 8 color codes with RGB values and usage context
- **7 pitfalls & gotchas**: Chatbot input interception when Ezra active, portfolio.json exists in both src/data and public/data, Terminal.tsx adds extra `terminal` property to TerminalWriter, BootOverlay uses direct DOM manipulation intentionally, snake game hijacks keyboard, CONFIG.commands is `as const` tuple, CSS import order determines specificity
- **What NOT to do table**: 15 explicit prohibitions (no jQuery, no CSS-in-JS, no Tailwind, no legacy imports, no CSS frameworks, no new pages, no var, no console.log, no innerHTML, etc.)
- **Manual testing checklists**: Quick smoke test (30 sec, 4 steps) and full regression test (5 min, 17 steps)

### 8. AGENT_HANDOFF.md (313 lines, 13.8 KB)
General agent handoff covering BOTH projects:
- **User profile**: Joel = 2nd-year CS @ VIT Chennai, prefers CLI-first, documentation-heavy, parallel subagents, easter eggs
- **Key accounts**: GitHub, HuggingFace, Devfolio, Google Skills, LinkedIn, Gmail, VIT email
- **LLM stack**: SambaNova/Xiaomi (free), GitHub Copilot, Ollama (local), Telegram gateway (planned)
- **System specs**: Windows 11 Pro x64, 64GB RAM, RTX 5070 (12GB VRAM), WSL2 with archlinux/Arch/kali/docker, Python 3.13, Node.js

**Project 1 — Portfolio** (`d:\portfolio-draft`): ✅ Functional, needs polish. Lists key files, 6 doc files, 8 personal database files, 2 AgentOS reference docs, remaining tasks

**Project 2 — AgentOS** (`d:\agentos`): 🔄 Partially built. 
- **DONE**: Router module (9 files, 100% complete — rules/semantic/LLM 3-layer cascade), 8 of 10 Hermes skills
- **NOT DONE**: 2 remaining skills (daily-briefing, git-commit), installer scripts (4 files, 0%), n8n workflows (4 JSON files, 0%), project docs (7 files, 0%)
- **How to continue**: Complete remaining skills, n8n workflows, installer scripts, test router

- **Architecture quick reference**: 3-layer router (rules 0ms → semantic 5-20ms → LLM 100-300ms), privacy override forces local Ollama
- **Port assignments**: LiteLLM :4000, Portfolio :4321, n8n :5678, Hermes :9119, Ollama :11434
- **Config locations**: ~/.hermes/ for Hermes, ~/.agentos/ for AgentOS
- **Full config schema**: YAML with identity, router, providers, privacy patterns, integrations (Telegram, email IMAP, n8n)
- **9 key design decisions**: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS in isolated WSL distro, LiteLLM as proxy, Semantic Router for Layer 2, n8n for automation, draft-only emails, SQLite for logs, Hermes v0.16.0, SKILL.md format
- **Open questions for Joel**: Telegram bot creation, Gmail 2FA/App Password, VIT email IMAP access, internship auto-apply confirmed draft-only

### 9. HERMES_REFERENCE.md (186 lines, 4.6 KB)
Technical reference for Hermes Agent v0.16.0:
- **Installation**: curl one-liner for Linux/WSL/macOS, irm for Windows PowerShell. Installs Python 3.11, Node.js, uv, ripgrep, ffmpeg
- **File layout**: ~/.hermes/ with config.yaml, .env, auth.json, skills/, memory/ (MEMORY.md 2200 char + USER.md 1375 char), sessions/ (SQLite + FTS5)
- **Complete CLI**: 20+ commands (chat, setup, model, gateway, dashboard, profile, config, auth, status, cron, tools, skills, mcp, memory, doctor, update, uninstall). Global flags: --tui, --profile, --resume, --continue, --verbose
- **Config example**: model (Claude Sonnet, OpenRouter), agent (90 max turns, medium reasoning), terminal backend options, memory, toolsets, skills external_dirs
- **Skill structure**: SKILL.md (required) with YAML frontmatter (name, description, version, author, license, platforms, hermes metadata tags/related/requires_tools) + optional scripts/, references/, templates/
- **Gateway setup (Telegram)**: 5 steps — @BotFather bot → hermes gateway setup → start → dashboard on :9119
- **70+ built-in tools**: Web (search, extract), Browser (navigate, click, snapshot, vision), Files (read, patch, terminal, process), Agents (todo, clarify, execute_code, delegate, memory, session_search, cronjob), Media (vision, image gen, video gen, TTS)
- **WSL2 fixes**: wsl.conf for metadata permissions and systemd

### 10. ROUTER_REFERENCE.md (108 lines, 3.9 KB)
Router architecture rationale and design decisions:
- **Why 3-layer**: Comparison table showing rules-only (60% accuracy), LLM-only (95% but slow+expensive), semantic-only (85%), vs hybrid (97% accuracy, 0-20ms avg, mostly free). 95% of traffic handled without LLM calls
- **Tools selected**: LiteLLM (unified proxy, failover/caching), Semantic Router (sub-20ms local), Pydantic (type safety)
- **Tools rejected**: RouteLLM (overkill for binary routing), TensorZero (archived, TOML config), Portkey (commercial), OpenRouter (managed, want self-hosted)
- **LiteLLM routing strategies**: simple-shuffle, least-busy, latency-based, cost-based, usage-based — but AgentOS uses custom 3-layer routing with LiteLLM as downstream proxy
- **Cost optimization insights**: RouteLLM reports 40-85% savings, semantic caching eliminates repeats, SambaNova is free for Joel, confidence-based cascading
- **Observability metrics** (SQLite): route distribution, latency per layer, cache hit rate, privacy override rate, provider fallback rate
- **Future**: Langfuse integration for production observability

---

## 🏗️ ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS EXTRACTED

1. **Astro Island Architecture**: Static HTML shell (zero JS) with selectively hydrated React islands (`client:load` for critical, `client:idle` for non-critical)
2. **Presentation/Logic Separation**: React handles rendering; pure TypeScript terminal engine is framework-agnostic
3. **TerminalWriter Abstraction**: Commands never touch xterm.js directly — enables testability and decoupling
4. **Command Registration Pattern**: Handler function → COMMANDS Map → CONFIG.commands array (for tab completion)
5. **Data Loading Strategy**: Async first load via fetch → module-level cache → sync getter for subsequent access
6. **3-Layer Routing Cascade** (AgentOS): Rules (0ms) → Semantic Embeddings (5-20ms) → LLM Classifier (100-300ms), with privacy override forcing local processing
7. **CSS Layer Architecture**: 5 ordered files with strict import sequence for specificity control
8. **Boot Sequence Gating**: Each phase (boot → lid → terminal → audio) gates the next via callbacks and CSS classes

## 🤝 AGENT HANDOFF PROTOCOLS

1. **8 Critical Rules** for AI agents (never modify legacy/, never import React in terminal engine, never use `any`, always update CONFIG.commands, etc.)
2. **File edit frequency map**: Most changes in src/terminal/, moderate in src/components/, rare in config files
3. **15 explicit prohibitions** (no jQuery, no Tailwind, no CSS-in-JS, no new pages, no var, no console.log, etc.)
4. **7 documented pitfalls**: chatbot input interception, dual portfolio.json locations, extra TerminalWriter property, DOM manipulation in BootOverlay, snake keyboard hijack, as const tuple type, CSS order sensitivity
5. **Manual testing checklists**: 30-second smoke test and 5-minute full regression
6. **Multi-project context**: Portfolio (functional) + AgentOS (partial) — continuation instructions for both

## 🔧 TECH DEBT ITEMS (14 total)

- **P0 Critical**: API key exposed client-side, no chatbot input sanitization
- **P1 Important**: No automated tests, duplicate TerminalTheme interface, no AI streaming, no left/right arrow keys, no mobile support, snake game hardcoded dimensions
- **P2 Nice-to-have**: No command history persistence, no data loading state, unskippable boot, no dark favicon, duplicate CONFIG export, no React Error Boundaries, CRT perf on low-end, no robots.txt/sitemap

## 📋 CONTRIBUTING GUIDELINES

- Conventional Commits, branch strategy (feat/fix/docs/refactor off main)
- Strict TypeScript, path aliases, function components, JSDoc on all exports
- Vanilla CSS only, CSS custom properties, prefers-reduced-motion respect
- PR template with What/Why/How/Testing/Screenshots
- Doc update requirements mapped to change types

## 🔧 MAINTENANCE PROCEDURES

- 12 step-by-step recipes covering all common tasks
- Deployment: static build → Vercel (recommended) or Netlify or manual
- Dependency updates with risk areas identified
- 7 troubleshooting scenarios with fixes
- Post-deploy verification checklist (8 items)

## 📁 FILE-BY-FILE SUMMARIES

- **Manual testing checklists**: Quick smoke test (30 sec, 4 steps) and full regression test (5 min, 17 steps)

### 8. AGENT_HANDOFF.md (313 lines, 13.8 KB)
General agent handoff covering BOTH projects:
- **User profile**: Joel = 2nd-year CS @ VIT Chennai, prefers CLI-first, documentation-heavy, parallel subagents, easter eggs

### 9. HERMES_REFERENCE.md (186 lines, 4.6 KB)

- **WSL2 fixes**: wsl.conf for metadata permissions and systemd

### 10. ROUTER_REFERENCE.md (108 lines, 3.9 KB)
Router architecture rationale and design decisions:
- **Why 3-layer**: Comparison table showing rules-only (60% accuracy), LLM-only (95% but slow+expensive), semantic-only (85%), vs hybrid (97% accuracy, 0-20ms avg, mostly free). 95% of traffic handled without LLM calls
- **Tools selected**: LiteLLM (unified proxy, failover/caching), Semantic Router (sub-20ms local), Pydantic (type safety)
- **Tools rejected**: RouteLLM (overkill for binary routing), TensorZero (archived, TOML config), Portkey (commercial), OpenRouter (managed, want self-hosted)
- **LiteLLM routing strategies**: simple-shuffle, least-busy, latency-based, cost-based, usage-based — but AgentOS uses custom 3-layer routing with LiteLLM as downstream proxy
- **Cost optimization insights**: RouteLLM reports 40-85% savings, semantic caching eliminates repeats, SambaNova is free for Joel, confidence-based cascading
- **Observability metrics** (SQLite): route distribution, latency per layer, cache hit rate, privacy override rate, provider fallback rate
- **Future**: Langfuse integration for production observability

## 🏗️ ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS EXTRACTED

1. **Astro Island Architecture**: Static HTML shell (zero JS) with selectively hydrated React islands (`client:load` for critical, `client:idle` for non-critical)
2. **Presentation/Logic Separation**: React handles rendering; pure TypeScript terminal engine is framework-agnostic
3. **TerminalWriter Abstraction**: Commands never touch xterm.js directly — enables testability and decoupling
4. **Command Registration Pattern**: Handler function → COMMANDS Map → CONFIG.commands array (for tab completion)
5. **Data Loading Strategy**: Async first load via fetch → module-level cache → sync getter for subsequent access
6. **3-Layer Routing Cascade** (AgentOS): Rules (0ms) → Semantic Embeddings (5-20ms) → LLM Classifier (100-300ms), with privacy override forcing local processing
7. **CSS Layer Architecture**: 5 ordered files with strict import sequence for specificity control
8. **Boot Sequence Gating**: Each phase (boot → lid → terminal → audio) gates the next via callbacks and CSS classes

## 🤝 AGENT HANDOFF PROTOCOLS

1. **8 Critical Rules** for AI agents (never modify legacy/, never import React in terminal engine, never use `any`, always update CONFIG.commands, etc.)
2. **File edit frequency map**: Most changes in src/terminal/, moderate in src/components/, rare in config files
3. **15 explicit prohibitions** (no jQuery, no Tailwind, no CSS-in-JS, no new pages, no var, no console.log, etc.)
4. **7 documented pitfalls**: chatbot input interception, dual portfolio.json locations, extra TerminalWriter property, DOM manipulation in BootOverlay, snake keyboard hijack, as const tuple type, CSS order sensitivity
5. **Manual testing checklists**: 30-second smoke test and 5-minute full regression
6. **Multi-project context**: Portfolio (functional) + AgentOS (partial) — continuation instructions for both

## 🔧 TECH DEBT ITEMS (14 total)

- **P0 Critical**: API key exposed client-side, no chatbot input sanitization
- **P1 Important**: No automated tests, duplicate TerminalTheme interface, no AI streaming, no left/right arrow keys, no mobile support, snake game hardcoded dimensions
- **P2 Nice-to-have**: No command history persistence, no data loading state, unskippable boot, no dark favicon, duplicate CONFIG export, no React Error Boundaries, CRT perf on low-end, no robots.txt/sitemap

## 📋 CONTRIBUTING GUIDELINES

- Conventional Commits, branch strategy (feat/fix/docs/refactor off main)
- Strict TypeScript, path aliases, function components, JSDoc on all exports
- Vanilla CSS only, CSS custom properties, prefers-reduced-motion respect
- PR template with What/Why/How/Testing/Screenshots
- Doc update requirements mapped to change types

## 🔧 MAINTENANCE PROCEDURES

- 12 step-by-step recipes covering all common tasks
- Deployment: static build → Vercel (recommended) or Netlify or manual
- Dependency updates with risk areas identified
- 7 troubleshooting scenarios with fixes
- Post-deploy verification checklist (8 items)
